title: Oregon DEQ to Update its Advanced Clean Truck Rule and Delay Start to Heavy-Duty Low NOx Omnibus Rule
content: TheÂ Oregon Department of Environmental QualityÂ is bringing temporary rules to the November Â Environmental Quality CommissionÂ meeting to adopt Californiaâ€™s expected minor amendments to itsÂ Advanced Clean TrucksÂ regulation.Â Oregonâ€™s Advanced Clean Truck RuleÂ was adopted in 2021 and is one of theÂ 13 states that has opted in to Californiaâ€™s vehicle and truck emission standards. Changes include adjustments to requirements for truck manufacturers, such as extending the deficit makeup period. Additionally, it makes clear compliance determination and sales reporting rules, which are defined when vehicles are produced and delivered for sale in the state. DEQ is seeking temporary rules to ensure these compliance updates are available for 2025 model year implementation. Changes include modifications for truck manufacturers, such as extending the deficit makeup period and clarifying compliance and sales reporting requirements based on when vehicles are produced and delivered in the state. The Department of Environmental Quality (DEQ) is pursuing temporary rules for these updates, effective for the 2025 model year. At the November Commission meeting, DEQ will also propose delaying the Heavy-Duty Low NOx Omnibus Rule start until 2026 due to a shortage of engines meeting the stricter emissions standards. Temporary rules adopted will take effect on January 1, 2025, while DEQ initiates permanent rulemaking. A public forum will be held to discuss these changes.
author: Mansift Kaur
